description_long: This object is a low, elongated stone barrier constructed from irregularly shaped rocks, tightly packed and mortared together. The stones vary in size and color, ranging from light gray to muted browns and subtle pinks, giving the structure a natural, rustic appearance. The top surface is uneven, with some stones protruding slightly above others, while the sides reveal the rough, textured faces of the rocks. The structure is relatively narrow in depth, suggesting it serves as a boundary or edging rather than a full walkway. Its weathered look indicates exposure to outdoor elements, typical of urban or park environments. The overall form is linear and solid, with no visible openings or interactive components.
description: A low, linear stone barrier made of irregular, mortared rocks with a rough, weathered surface and natural color variation.
description_view_0: Front view shows the full length of the stone barrier, highlighting the irregular stone arrangement and color variation.
description_view_1: Side view reveals the narrow depth and the stacked, uneven profile of the stones.
description_view_2: Top view displays the uneven, jagged surface of the stones forming the upper edge.
description_view_3: Opposite side view again shows the narrow profile and the rough, natural stone faces.
